company.name: Meshy
company.tagline: A Tsinghua Yao-class to MIT graphics PhD built the GPU language behind 3D-AI, then quietly productized it — 60 percent Western share, 40 million ARR, and no canonical funding wire
company.summary: Meshy is the cleanest current case for an academic-graphics-substrate playbook. Founder Yuanming (Ethan) Hu built Taichi Lang — a 27K-star differentiable GPU language for visual computing — across his MIT PhD years, then in April 2023 quietly pivoted his pre-existing Taichi.graphics company into Meshy, a productized text-to-3D pipeline. Three years later: 10 million users, 100 million generated assets, 40 million ARR (April 2026), 85 percent gross margin, roughly 60 percent Western 3D-generative market share — with no canonical TechCrunch funding announcement and a deliberately quiet GTM posture. Sequoia and GGV-backed, but it acts bootstrapped in PR. The unusual move is the inversion of the Manus playbook: same bicultural precondition, opposite operational choice.

events.title: Yuanming Hu starts MIT PhD
events.description: Yuanming (Ethan) Hu enrolls at MIT EECS / CSAIL under Frédo Durand and Bill Freeman, two of the most influential names in computer graphics. The substrate phase begins eight years before Meshy's operational launch.

events.title: Taichi Lang first ACM TOG paper
events.description: The Taichi GPU programming language for sparse and quantized visual computing publishes at ACM Transactions on Graphics. The academic substrate that becomes Meshy's technical foundation lands in the public record.

events.title: Hu defends MIT PhD thesis (Taichi)
events.description: MIT thesis on Taichi published. Earns honorable mention for SIGGRAPH 2022 outstanding doctoral dissertation. Taichi Lang itself crosses 27K GitHub stars across 300-plus institutional adopters.

events.title: Taichi.graphics legal entity
events.description: Crunchbase records Meshy LLC entity formation in 2021 — operating as Taichi.graphics, the predecessor company built around the open-source GPU language. The pivot to a productized 3D-gen company comes 18 months later.

events.title: Meshy Texturer launches
events.description: First public-facing Meshy-branded tool: a web utility for AI texture generation on existing 3D meshes. Pre-product probe of demand in the gamedev / 3D-asset community.

events.title: Meshy company operational launch
events.description: Hu effectively starts Meshy as a product company in April 2023. Anchor: his August 22, 2024 X post — 'We started working on Meshy 16 months ago.' This is the operational founding, not the 2021 legal entity.

events.title: Meshy-1 release
events.description: First full text-to-3D model generation product. Marketed as 30x faster than the prevailing AI methods. Launches on Product Hunt and lands on early gamedev community comparison sites.

events.title: Meshy 2 / 2.5 enhancements
events.description: Iteration cadence — quality and speed improvements to text-to-3D. Establishes the version-cadence-as-format pattern that becomes the GTM. Each Meshy N+1 must visibly outperform N or community comparison sites move on.

events.title: Series A reported (no canonical wire)
events.description: Approximately 10-15 million Series A reported via aggregators (Tracxn, parsers.vc). Sequoia and GGV among investors per third-party data. No canonical TechCrunch / Bloomberg / Information piece exists. The PR posture starts here: quiet C, loud product.

events.title: Meshy 3 launch
events.description: Adds image-to-3D, sculptures, and PBR materials. Marks the first anniversary of operational founding. Image-to-3D is the boundary push — same SKU now serves designers without a text-prompting workflow.

events.title: Meshy 4 launch
events.description: Reworked architecture with separate Modeling and Texturing stages. Hu posts on X: 'Sooo proud of everyone on the Meshy team' — the 16-months-since-start framing that pins operational founding to April 2023.

events.title: a16z Games top tools list
events.description: Recognized as #1 in popularity among 3D AI tools by a16z Games' annual list. KOL-tier industry validation that does heavy lifting in the absence of canonical funding press.

events.title: Meshy 5 Preview + animation library
events.description: 500+ character animations, smarter image-to-3D alignment, cleaner geometry. Public disclosure: 3 million users, 30 million assets generated. Animation library is the boundary push from static asset generation into rigged, ready-to-deploy game characters.

events.title: Meshy 6 Preview
events.description: Major mesh quality and geometric accuracy lift, especially for organic characters and anatomy. Sets up the November ARR disclosure window.

events.title: $15M ARR + 30% MoM disclosure
events.description: Self-reported via PR wire. 30 percent month-over-month growth. Top 2 percent fastest-growing AI in Silicon Valley. 85 percent gross margin. ~60 percent Western 3D-gen market share. Bundled with Meshy 6 Preview — the canonical Meshy milestone format: ARR + product, no funding round.

events.title: GDC 2026 — Meshy Labs + $30M ARR
events.description: Meshy Labs experimental incubator launches with first AI-native game 'Black Box: Infinite Arsenal'. ARR doubles in three months ($15M to $30M). 10M+ users, 100M+ assets. The category-defining moment: gamedev now treats Meshy as a platform, not a tool.

events.title: 36Kr feature: $40M ARR / 60% Western share
events.description: Chinese-language flagship coverage. ARR ≈ ¥300M RMB (~$40M USD; the '$300M' headline framing is a translation artifact). Monthly visits >5M per SimilarWeb. Frames Meshy as a Silicon Valley unicorn, not a Chinese-team-going-global story.

events.title: Formlabs partnership / RAPID + TCT 2026
events.description: Audience boundary push from gamedev into pro 3D printing and manufacturing. Meshy debuts a Formlabs bridge at RAPID + TCT 2026 — same SKU, new buyer category. The TAM expands without the product expanding.

arr.source: Meshy 5 Preview disclosure (users only — no ARR)

valuation.round: Series A (estimate; aggregator-derived, no canonical wire)

platforms.name: X (Twitter)
platforms.description: Yuanming Hu posts on X (~16K followers) primarily about Meshy releases and Taichi. Deliberately non-loud — closer to Linear's Karri Saarinen than to Replit's Amjad Masad. The thought-leadership weight is delegated to the technical artifacts (Taichi GitHub, MIT thesis, SIGGRAPH paper) rather than to daily X presence.
platforms.catalyst: Hu's August 22, 2024 X post: 'We started working on Meshy 16 months ago.' One sentence on one timeline that pins the operational founding date and reframes the 2021 Crunchbase record. The substrate-coded launch post — no demo video, no fanfare, just the version number and a thank-you to the team.
platforms.whenItWorks: When the founder substrate is already deep enough that X is a confirmation channel, not an acquisition channel. Taichi Lang's 27K stars do the heavy lifting — X just confirms which company is shipping next.
platforms.whenItDoesnt: If you don't have a substrate, X presence alone won't manufacture one. Daily posting without underlying technical artifacts decays into noise.

platforms.name: GitHub
platforms.role: The substrate that makes Meshy permitted to exist
platforms.description: Taichi Lang (taichi-dev/taichi) is a 27K-star differentiable GPU programming language for sparse and quantized visual computing. Hu created and maintained it across his MIT PhD. 300-plus institutions use it in production. None of Meshy's 3D-gen competitors have an equivalent substrate — and that asymmetry is the GTM.
platforms.catalyst: Taichi Lang crossing 25K GitHub stars in 2022 — a year before Meshy's operational launch. The substrate was already deep before the productization began. By the time Meshy 1 shipped in October 2023, every technical evaluator in 3D-gen who had heard of Taichi already had a default-positive prior on the founder.
platforms.whenItWorks: When the open-source artifact is technically adjacent to the productized SaaS — Taichi's differentiable simulation maps onto diffusion-on-3D-meshes. Substrate that maps onto product is the rarest and most valuable kind.
platforms.whenItDoesnt: If the open-source repo is a tangent (a side-project marketing tool), it does not buy substrate credit. The repo has to be the engine, not the brochure.

platforms.name: YouTube
platforms.role: Long-form technical proof for the skeptics
platforms.description: Hu has done at least one founder-podcast (XR AI Spotlight) and one Chinese-language podcast (WhynotTV / Tairan He). Long-form YouTube is where the 'is this just another diffusion wrapper?' debate gets answered with an actual founder voice steeped in differential geometry.
platforms.catalyst: XR AI Spotlight: The Future of 3D Generative AI with Meshy's CEO Ethan Hu. The first long-form English-language Meshy interview that lets a 3D-gen-curious audience hear Hu's MIT-graphics-substrate-meets-diffusion thesis directly.
platforms.whenItWorks: When the founder can speak fluently to the technical depth behind the product. A 3D-AI founder with a graphics PhD has different long-form leverage than a wrapper-layer founder.
platforms.whenItDoesnt: Long-form is unforgiving when the founder cannot defend the architectural claims. Skip it if the substrate is shallow.

platforms.name: Discord
platforms.role: Gamedev workflow community + power-user feedback loop
platforms.description: Meshy's Discord is the gathering point for indie gamedevs, Blender / Unreal / Unity / Roblox / Godot bridge users, and 3D printing experimenters. Comparison content lives here — version-by-version side-by-sides between Meshy, Tripo, Luma's Genie, and Rodin. The community does the unpaid distribution.
platforms.catalyst: Meshy 5 Preview animation library reveal (Jul 2025) — the 500+ character animations turned the Discord from a feedback channel into a content-creation flywheel. Users posted rigged-and-animated character demos within 48 hours of release.
platforms.whenItWorks: When the product output is shareable and the user base has a strong community comparison instinct (gamedev, music, video). Discord rewards live demos and version comparisons.
platforms.whenItDoesnt: Discord is wrong for B2B tools where the buyer is a procurement officer, not a power user.

platforms.name: 小红书 (Xiaohongshu)
platforms.description: Chinese 3D-print and e-commerce communities post Meshy workflows on 小红书 — print-this-figurine, design-this-product-render content. The company does not market here directly, but the bicultural team's substrate (Taichi Lang's Chinese open-source community) seeds organic adoption.
platforms.catalyst: No single moment. The slow-burn presence of Chinese gamedev, 3D-print, and animation creators sharing Meshy outputs. Cross-platform with 抖音 (Douyin) and Bilibili.
platforms.whenItWorks: When a bicultural team has organic Chinese reach without needing to position the company as Chinese-origin. Substrate-coded distribution.
platforms.whenItDoesnt: If the company brand is fully Western-positioned, leaning into 小红书 marketing risks resurfacing the geo-arbitrage frame Meshy chose to suppress.

platforms.name: Reddit
platforms.description: r/blender, r/Unity3D, r/UnrealEngine, r/3Dprinting are where the head-to-head comparisons against Tripo, Luma, Rodin, and Common Sense Machines play out. Reddit threads function as the unofficial benchmark archive for 3D-gen tools.
platforms.catalyst: Recurring comparison threads after every Meshy version release. The community-comparison-as-distribution mechanism: users comparison-shop in public, Meshy keeps winning, the threads become the marketing without a marketing budget.
platforms.whenItWorks: When the category has a single canonical comparison standard (mesh quality + topology + animation rigging + export support). Reddit rewards reproducible benchmarks.
platforms.whenItDoesnt: If the company seeds threads, the community detects astroturfing inside a week. Stay community-led or stay off.
